occurrence: I dispatched two subagents in parallel: 'Phase 1 RestGitHubClient' and 'Phase 0+2 agent harness'. They worked on disjoint files (rest_client.py vs agent_lib/) but on the same branch. Phase 1 finished first and pushed; Phase 0 had locally-uncommitted files when Phase 1 ran git status, leading to a moment of confusion ('untracked agent_lib/ from concurrent agent').
lesson: Parallel subagents on the same branch must coordinate their commit timing. The skill should give each subagent its OWN sub-branch so they can commit independently.

context: user explaining the recurring task they want as a skill
content: We also need a skill that encapsulates the workflow 'create branch, create plan as main agent, package self-contained specs for subagents, create sub-branch for each subagent, dispatch and wait, merge subagent branches into branch and reconcile, then main agent creates PR with cumulative work, and includes a run report'. I often want the agent to do this, but it is a lot of typing each time.

occurrence: Test-writer subagent received an 800-word self-contained brief covering 8 test files, ~40 test cases, fixtures, conftest, pytest.ini path tweaks. It produced 67 passing tests in one shot. The brief was reused (with edits) for iter 2's test-expansion subagent.
lesson: Subagent briefs ARE reusable templates if you parameterize the variable bits. The skill should template the brief from a Plan dict, so the dispatcher writes the goal once and the brief is generated.

content: The spec proposed `<feature>/sub-<id>` as the subagent branch pattern. GitHub returned 422 when I tried to create `agent/harness-01-X/sub-alpha` under existing `agent/harness-01-X` because git refs cannot have a name and a sub-namespace at the same prefix level.
fix: Changed separator to `--sub-` (double-dash). The skill MUST use this separator by default; making it a parameter creates a footgun.

occurrence: Subagent driving 28-branch cleanup pushed PRs sequentially because it didn't realize close_on_merge could process them concurrently. ~12 minutes of serial PR-merge-PR-merge work.
lesson: The skill's cleanup step (post-merge) should also be parallelizable. Even if the workflow itself sweeps each branch on its PR's merge, the dispatcher can fire all the merges in parallel and let GitHub handle the queueing.

content: When merging multiple sub-branches into the feature branch, do it sequentially in plan-order (NOT in subagent-completion-order). This makes conflicts deterministic and the run-report's subtask-table align with the merge results.
lesson: The skill should record completion order separately from merge order; merge order = plan order.

content: The run report should live BOTH in the PR body AND as a committed file at harness/runs/<run_id>/report.md. The PR body is for review; the committed copy survives PR closure and is grep-able later.
lesson: Always write the run report to a file in addition to the PR body.
